subject: Re: tilt handedness
body:
Hello Alister,
Thank you very much for your swift and insightful suggestions! I always 
appreciate your generosity in sharing your expertise with all of us. 

As precise as possible to use Warp CTF estimation, I again measured CTF 
after verification of tilt handedness.
I believe this is the best way to use Warp CTF estimation, as you mentioned 
above. Let me know if I miss something.

I use Warp reconstruction to use deconvoluted tomograms for particle 
picking/Filament tracing using Amira. As you said, I can switch to Relion 
reconstructed tomogram to crop particles for Dynamo alignment. So far, I 
have not encountered a problem shifting the Warp-reconstructed tomogram to 
the Relion-reconstructed tomogram after dynamo alignment based on one test 
tomogram.
But indeed, I should make the processing pipeline as simple as possible!
Thanks again, and have a wonderful evening!
Reika 
On Wednesday, December 21, 2022 at 2:14:49 AM UTC-8 alist...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hi Reika,
>
> Kino’s conversion tool takes per-image defocus values and the tilt-series 
> alignment parameters from the tilt-series xml files, they should be as 
> correct as possible in Warp prior to conversion for the best results. There 
> are no provisions in RELION 4.0 for estimating CTF parameters.
>
> If you’re aiming to use RELION and picking with Dynamo I would recommend...
> - converting from warp to RELION
> - generating tomograms in RELION 
> - picking particles in those tomograms in Dynamo
> - realigning in Dynamo
> - converting back to RELION
>
> This will minimise headaches and necessary debugging :)
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 21 Dec 2022, at 05:13, Reika Watanabe <reika.wa...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Dear all,
> I am using Warp/M 1.0.9 to preprocess the tilt series and reconstruction 
> with IMOD alignment.
> After reconstruction with Warp and further processing with Dynamo, I just 
> realized that I had missed the "Check tilt handedness" step for all 
> tomogram data, and they needed to be inverted. 
> I did invert through "Check tilt handedness" and reestimate CTF after 
> inverted. This step created updated XML files for all tilt series data.
> After using Dynamo pre-aligned data (converted using dynamo2war and 
> startools), I will go on Relion 4 using Warp2reliontomo.
>
> Since CTF estimation was not used, now using the un_alligned tilt series 
> (created before inversion) and new XML file,  I assume that CTF estimation 
> is correctly implemented for processing relion 4. Please correct me if I am 
> wrong...
>
> I appreciate your kind thoughts; thank you very much in advance! 
> Reika 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
